lenses.guidance: God renames Jacob Israel at v 10 — a public confirmation of the Peniel naming (32:28) — and self-discloses as El Shaddai at v 11. The Hebrew formula links name and covenant promise: identity given, fruitfulness commanded.

The return to Bethel (vv 1, 7) closes the pilgrimage opened at 28:10-22: same God, same place, same covenant promise — now spoken to a renamed man with a household in tow. The arc of departure and return is complete.

The pillar at Bethel (v 14) is later corrupted by Jeroboam's calf at the same site (1 Kgs 12:28-29) and denounced by Hosea (Hos 4:15). Bethel becomes a thread of fidelity and apostasy running throughout Scripture.

'Put away the foreign gods that are among you' (v 2) precedes the worship at Bethel. Repent before you build the altar — household idols (vv 2-4) buried under the oak make space for a genuine encounter with God.
